summary: Policy-backed control for notification mirroring across devices. The local Microsoft WPN.admx file explicitly documents the same user policy key, value name, and enabled/disabled values that the app uses.

setting.name: Notification Mirroring
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can mirror some notifications to other devices. This policy decides whether that cross-device mirroring is allowed for the current user.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable notification mirroring for privacy, to reduce cross-device noise, or because they do not want Windows pushing alerts between devices.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning this off improves privacy and keeps notifications local, but mirrored notifications will no longer show up on other devices.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents NoNotificationMirroring in the ADMX_WPN Policy CSP. The app uses the user-side policy path HKCU\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PushNotifications\DisallowNotificationMirroring = 1, which matches the documented disable behavior.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Notification mirroring follows normal Windows behavior.

allowed_values.label: Notification mirroring allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and notifications can still be mirrored to other devices.

allowed_values.label: Notification mirroring turned off
allowed_values.meaning: Notifications are not mirrored to other devices for the current user.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisallowNotificationMirroring = 1 for the current user, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows user-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Supported Windows 10 and Windows 11 editions with notification mirroring policy support

states.rationale: The user policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: Microsoft documents the functional default as notification mirroring allowed when the policy is not configured.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who want standard cross-device notification behavior
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep standard mirrored-notification behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to keep notifications local to the current device.
tradeoffs: Notifications can continue crossing to other devices.

recommended_profiles.label: Local-only notifications
intended_for: Users who do not want mirrored alerts
avoid_for: Users who rely on cross-device notification flow

states.rationale: Block notifications from being mirrored to other devices.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want alerts to stay on the current device only.
tradeoffs: The setup becomes more private and less noisy across devices.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: ADMX_WPN / NoNotificationMirroring
evidence.summary: Official Microsoft documentation for the policy that turns off notification mirroring.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WPN.admx NoNotificationMirroring mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps NoNotificationMirroring to the PushNotifications policy key with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WPN.adml NoNotificationMirroring help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy turns off notification mirroring.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisallowNotificationMirroring = 1 for the current tweak.

decision.why: The local Microsoft WPN.admx file explicitly defines NoNotificationMirroring at SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PushNotifications with valueName DisallowNotificationMirroring and enabledValue 1 / disabledValue 0, which matches the app's user policy surface.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same user policy key and value name that the app writes and confirms the 1/0 mapping.
